questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: An ideal index (guide) fossil should have
options: wide geographic range and short time range
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Good index fossils are widespread but short-lived (fine biostratigraphic resolution).

**Working.**
Wide area, short time.

**Answer.** Wide range, short duration

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Trilobites are an extinct class of arthropods that were most abundant in the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trilobites flourished in the Paleozoic and died out at the end-Permian.

**Working.**
Paleozoic.

**Answer.** Paleozoic

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Ammonoids (ammonites) are key Mesozoic index fossils belonging to the phylum
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ammonoids are cephalopod molluscs.

**Working.**
Mollusca.

**Answer.** Mollusca

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Which statements about the end-Cretaceous (K–Pg) extinction are correct?
options: It is associated with an iridium anomaly
questions.solution:
**Concept.** K–Pg (~66 Ma) killed non-avian dinosaurs and has an Ir anomaly; trilobites died at the end-Permian (~252 Ma).

**Working.**
Options 1, 2, 3.

**Answer.** K–Pg facts

questions.subject: Palaeontology
questions.stem: Foraminifera are widely used in petroleum biostratigraphy because they are
options: abundant calcareous microfossils with rapid evolution
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Forams are abundant, environmentally sensitive, fast-evolving microfossils.

**Working.**
Calcareous microfossils.

**Answer.** Calcareous microfossils
